Rahimi genuinely loves people and adores every single one of his patients. At Fusion Orthodontics, we pride ourselves on providing the latest, highest quality, affordable orthodontic care in all of Plano / North Dallas. Dr. Rahimi specializes in Brava by BRIUS, Invisalign, and traditional braces such as metal and ceramic. Most people regret not straightening their teeth sooner to enjoy smiling – see Dr. Rahimi. He will turn your dream smile into a reality.

More About Brava Invisalign Invisalign is an orthodontic system that gradually moves teeth using clear plastic appliances called “aligner trays. ” This teeth straightening treatment works through a series of invisible, removable, and comfortable aligners that no one can tell you’re wearing. At Fusion, we can give you the beautiful straight teeth you’ve always wanted, and you don’t have to wait until the treatment is over to start smiling.

At Fusion, patients can choose clear braces or Clarity Advanced Flash Free brackets from 3M if seeking discretion. If you want invisible braces, then getting ceramic braces is the right choice. More About Clear Braces At Fusion, we don’t shy away from any case, no matter its difficulty. To Give our patients the smile they deserve gives us nothing but joy. That is why we offer orthodontics in our Plano / North Dallas office to all ages: kids, teens, adults, and seniors.

Orthodontic Services We Offer In Plano Brava by BRIUS Brava is the most advanced and revolutionary orthodontic system that moves each tooth simultaneously and independently from one another, shortening treatment time up to 50-75%. In addition, Brava is also lingual, which means you can get your smile discreetly. This orthodontic system is the most hygienic in the market and makes visits to the orthodontist a thing of the past.

Hessam Rahimi Board Certified Orthodontist DDS, DMSc, MBA Looking for a reliable and professional orthodontist in Plano TX. Our practice has got you covered. You can trust us to provide top-of-the-line dental care with state-of-the-art orthodontic technology. Plus, we make it easy on your wallet with affordable monthly payment plans and we accept all major PPO insurances. Interested in our Invisalign, Brava, or Traditional Braces plans. Get all the details today.

The treatment process is carried out using clear, custom-made aligners that fit snugly over the patient's teeth. These are changed every two weeks and gradually move the teeth into the desired position. More About Invisalign Metal braces are a type of orthodontic treatment used to straighten teeth and correct misalignment. They consist of brackets made of metal or ceramic, which are attached to each tooth with an adhesive.

A wire is threaded through the brackets, which is then tightened by the orthodontist at regular intervals to slowly move the teeth into their desired position. More About Metal Braces Clear braces are an increasingly popular alternative to traditional metal braces for both children and adults. Clear braces, sometimes referred to as ceramic braces or invisible braces, offer an aesthetic solution that corrects the misalignment of teeth without using conspicuous metal brackets.

We keep our patients and parents entertained to make the experience as pleasant as possible. We Are Green We take pride in trying to be as GREEN as possible and leave the smallest carbon footprint. Rahimi is a premier provider of Brava™ lingual orthodontic system with the highest number of treated cases around the world. He routinely lectures about this technology nationally and internationally and he trains other orthodontists to learn how to work with the system. Lingual braces are positioned behind the teeth to keep them out of sight, but not many orthodontists offer the service due to its challenging nature for the provider, a problem we don’t have at Fusion orthodontics. ‍ Dr.

Rahimi performs orthodontic treatment for all ages from 7 years for screening all the way to adults , children, and adults, including special needs children with conditions such as developmental anomalies, neurodivergence, cleft lip, and palate, autism, and a multitude of genetic disorders. Dr. Rahimi also treats patients that require jaw surgery (aka. Orthognathic Surgery) in collaboration with several oral surgeons in the Greater Dallas area. Dr. Rahimi is the founder of www. identists.

Rahimi is a certified Celebrace (metal) and Lightforce (clear) custom braces provider, a new trend in orthodontics that allows further customization of the orthodontic treatment with the goal of reducing the total treatment time and the number of visits. Dr. Rahimi is certified to provide clear aligner appliances such as Invisalign® to treat patients that don’t like the appearance and discomfort of regular conventional braces.

He is an amazing young man that his spirit and strong will is inspirational to everyone that knows him. ‍ Dr. Rahimi and his wife Dr. Niloo from Fusion Children’s Dentistry have two children, Ava and Arman, and they live walking distance to the Dallas office. If you saw him at the Lifetime™ gym at Preston, or the Tennis courts at Lifetime™ or Fretz, make sure to have your retainer if you decide to say hello (JK. ).

Rahimi is also a proud alumnus of The University of Texas at Dallas where he received his Master of Business Administration (MBA) degree in 2016. Dr. Rahimi has established Fusion dental research center next to the clinical facility through which he participates in clinical research and education of the orthodontic community.

Hessam Rahimi is a Diplomate of The American Board of Orthodontics (ABO) who received his orthodontic training at Harvard University in addition to receiving a Doctorate degree in Oral Biology in 2011. After moving to Dallas in 2011 with his family, he established Fusion Orthodontics in two locations in Dallas and downtown Garland, and since then, he has treated more than 10,000 patients, giving them the beautiful smile they deserve while maintaining a 5-star reputation. As you replace each aligner with the next in the series, your teeth will move – little by little, week by week – until they have straightened to their final positions with the simple guidelines you will be given. Successful treatment requires compliance with following these simple guidelines. You’ll visit Fusion Orthodontics about once every six weeks to ensure that your treatment is progressing as planned.

Invisalign in Plano, TX Certified Invisalign Provider Having a beautiful, straighter smile without the hassle of braces is now possible with Invisalign. Stop by to see our orthodontist in Plano. During your consultation, we can find out if these invisible aligners are the right fit for you. What is Invisalign. Invisalign is a series of clear, removable, and comfortable aligners that are barely noticeable.

And no metal and wires usually mean you spend less time in your doctor’s office getting adjustments. Invisalign also allows you to view your own virtual treatment plan when you start so you can see how your straight teeth will look when your treatment is complete. How Does Invisalign Work. You wear each set of teeth aligners for about two weeks, removing them only to eat, drink, brush, and floss.

Not only will you enjoy budget-friendly monthly prices, but we also have some fantastic promotions lined up just for you. The series of aligners will take your teeth from their current position to their final desired alignment, which is determined by Dr. Rahimi, one step at a time. Why Would I Want Invisalign. Not only are the aligners invisible, but they are also removable, so you can eat and drink what you want while in treatment. Plus, brushing and flossing are no problem, as the aligners are removable. They are also comfortable, with no metal to cause mouth abrasions during treatment.

During your consultation, we'll evaluate whether clear braces are right for you. If so, special dental cement is used to attach the brackets to the front of your teeth. Then, clear elastic ligatures attach the wire to the brackets, which gradually move your teeth by applying pressure to your periodontal ligaments.

Metal Braces Braces can seem daunting, but recent technological advancements have made them more lightweight, comfortable, and better-looking than ever. These modern braces are created with high-grade stainless steel and attach to teeth using a special cement. Each bracket connects to an archwire that applies pressure to shift teeth into their new positions.

Once you achieve your ideal alignment, the wires then serve to hold your teeth stable while your jawbone and ligaments heal. With clear braces, you can achieve a confident and beautiful smile without the visibility of traditional metal braces. Will you go for clear or metal braces. Reserve an Appointment.

Brace costs are a maze, differing between offices and patients. At Fusion Orthodontics, braces can cost from $2,500 for short early interventions (Phase 1), to $8,000 for more complex and challenging cases (e. g. surgical). Multiple factors affect the pricing of orthodontic services: The technology of choice and appliances Difficulty of treatment Duration of treatment Dental Insurance Private Corporate Dentistry Added upgrades (Retainer Advantage Plan, Clear Ceramic Braces, etc.

Our Payment Platform We use Orthobanc to automate payment in our collection system. So after the contract starts, you will not deal with us directly. Orthobanc will handle the financial transactions of the contract.

So, if your treatment is $5,000, they'd theoretically cover $2,500 (50% of $5,000), but their lifetime maximum kicks in at $1,500. That means they'll only pay $1,500, leaving you with a $3,500 balance. Now, for a smaller treatment costing $2,000, they won't cover the full $1,500 lifetime maximum. They'll cover 50%, which is $1,000, leaving you responsible for the remaining $1,000. Confusing, right. You're not alone.

Placed behind the teeth so they cannot be seen, this method considerably shortens treatment time, allows for easier cleaning than traditional braces, and is more comfortable than braces or aligners throughout treatment. Clear Braces We use 3M’s Clarity Advanced brackets. These are one of the most discrete brackets available and in combination with clear wires, they can get quite invisible.

Medicaid and Braces Medicaid either pays for your braces treatment down to the last penny or doesn’t pay a dime. To qualify, you need to first meet their minimum requirements that differ from state to state and from time to time. In Texas, up until 2012, it was very easy to get approved under the Medicaid program, but then investigations indicated that the state of Texas paid more than the rest of the country for braces treatment (about 300 million dollars in 2010).

FBI got involved and some corporations and individuals got indicted for fraud and other criminal acts; the drama leads to an overhaul of the approval process for such patients. According to the most recent changes, to get approved, the patient has to have a minimum of 4 severe problems out of a list of 10. If your teeth look kind of ok, the bets are that you will not meet those minimum requirements.

We offer automatic payments with credit cards and bank accounts so you don’t have to worry about making the payments every month at our counter. We are very flexible when it comes to arranging the payment plan in a way that would meet your financial situation. We offer long-term payment plans with zero interest for some patients. We also offer a pay-in-full discount. To know more, feel free to reach out to us to discuss your financial needs.

The Process At Fusion, after you fill out the necessary forms that are pretty much standard across different dental offices, you will sit down with Dr. Rahimi. Remember at Fusion, consultations for braces are always available. Dr. Rahimi will evaluate your teeth and address your orthodontic concerns. He will explain how long the treatment will take, and what technologies are best for the treatment.

Anyhow, the only way to know for sure is to visit an orthodontist who accepts Medicaid to figure out if you can qualify. Please note that the CHIP program doesn’t cover braces. HMO Insurance and Braces These insurances usually offer a discount for the members on the regular fee the orthodontist charges. For example, the HMO insurance will mention a 25% discount on the regular fee when it comes to orthodontic services.

) Private (PPO) Insurance and Braces Not every PPO insurance covers orthodontics. Some policies have restrictions, like age limits. If you don't meet these criteria, they won't cover orthodontic benefits. Keep in mind, these limits can vary between insurance carriers. PPO Insurance can be a bit tricky. Let's break it down: they set a lifetime maximum (let's say $1,500) and a percentage of treatment cost (like 50%).

Based on the information he provides, the treatment coordinator will come up with the exact cost of the treatment and then he will sit down with you to work out a payment arrangement that will work best for you. The treatment coordinator will also introduce our treatment options to you and some packages we offer. Orthodontic Packages: Brava by Brius Brava by Brius is a revolutionary method that can be used instead of braces or aligners that moves the teeth independently.

When things get a bit complex or teeth need a significant shift, we might have to add visible attachments. However, for the right candidates, Clear Aligners are a game-changer. Just a heads up, Invisalign cases come with a premium charge, and there's a 4 to 6 weeks waiting period to get those sleek appliances back from the lab. Metal Braces ‍ Our most cost-effective, straightforward, and convenient option is regular metal braces. We use top-of-the-line Victory brackets by 3M.

Upon arriving, each patient and parent will be seen by the staff and doctor who will acclimate you to our office and prepare for the initial exam. We will take the necessary photographs and X-rays to allow us to make a proper diagnosis. The doctor will then complete a brief, but thorough, exam. What will I learn from the initial examination. * Is there an orthodontic problem, and if so, what is it. * What must be done to correct the problem. * Will any teeth need to be removed.

The space available for the front teeth does not increase as you grow. In most people, after the permanent molars erupt, the space available for the front teeth decreases with age.

Phase One treatment, if necessary, is usually initiated on children between the ages of 7 and 10. Phase One treatment usually lasts about 16-21 months.

The primary objective for Phase One treatment is to address significant problems to prevent them from becoming more severe and to improve self-esteem and self-image. Will my child need full braces if he/she has Phase One treatment. It is best to assume that your child will need full braces even after Phase One treatment. The period following Phase One treatment is called the “resting period,” during which growth and tooth eruption are closely monitored.

If your braces are causing extreme pain or if something breaks, you should call our office. In most cases, we can address these issues over the telephone. Patients should brush their teeth at least four times each day – after each meal and before going to bed.
